title_statement.title: Europe, Middle East, and North Africa region population projections : 1988-89 edition

document_description.abstract: Population projections are provided in this paper for the individual countries comprising EMENA region. The projections cover the period 1985-2150. The length of the projection period was chosen to allow countries to approach stability, which for several is projected to take as long as two centuries. The paper begins with an introduction which explains what projection results are provided in the detailed tables, describes the projection methodology, and summarizes and interprets the main results.
document_description.spatial_coverage: Europe and Central Asia ; Middle East and North Africa
document_description.usage_terms: See World Bank Access to Information (https://www.worldbank.org/en/access-to-information)
document_description.disclaimer: The Policy Research Working Paper Series disseminates the findings of work in progress to encourage the exchange of ideas about development issues. An objective of the series is to get the findings out quickly, even if the presentations are less than fully polished. The papers carry the names of the authors and should be cited accordingly. The findings, interpretations, and conclusions expressed in this paper are entirely those of the authors.
